definition: The state of feeling or showing pleasure or contentment.
example: Happiness can be found, even in the darkest of times, if one only remembers to turn on the light.
speech: Great Hall address after Black's escape

definition: With little or no light; morally grim.
example: Happiness can be found, even in the darkest of times, if one only remembers to turn on the light.
speech: Great Hall address after Black's escape

example: Happiness can be found, even in the darkest of times, if one only remembers to turn on the light.
speech: Great Hall address after Black's escape

definition: An act of selecting between possibilities; the seat of moral character.
example: It is our choices, Harry, that show what we truly are, far more than our abilities.

definition: Possession of the means or skill to do something.
example: It is our choices, Harry, that show what we truly are, far more than our ability alone.

definition: An intense feeling of deep affection; a protective force in the story's moral physics.
example: Do not pity the dead, Harry. Pity the living, and, above all, those who live without love.

definition: The feeling of sorrow and compassion caused by others' suffering.
example: Do not pity the dead, Harry. Pity the living, and, above all, those who live without love.

definition: An unpleasant emotion caused by threat or danger.
example: It is the unknown we fear when we look upon death and darkness, nothing more.

definition: The end of life; a teacher of perspective.
example: Do not pity the dead, Harry. Pity the living—and remember that to the well-organized mind, death is but the next great adventure.

definition: An unusual and exciting experience; a journey into risk.
example: To the well-organized mind, death is but the next great adventure.

definition: A single distinct unit of language; speech as power.
example: Words are, in my not-so-humble opinion, our most inexhaustible source of magic.

definition: The power of influencing events by supernatural means; metaphorically, transformative speech.
example: Words are, in my not-so-humble opinion, our most inexhaustible source of magic.

definition: Make it easier for someone to do something; give assistance.
example: Help will always be given at Hogwarts to those who ask for it.
source: Chamber of Secrets / Order of the Phoenix themes

definition: A point or way in which people or things are not the same.
example: We are only as strong as we are united, as weak as we are divided—indifference to one another is a kind of difference that wounds.

definition: Joined together politically or for a common purpose.
example: We are only as strong as we are united, as weak as we are divided.

definition: A feeling of expectation and desire for a certain thing to happen.
example: Happiness can be found even in dark times—hope is the hand that finds the switch.
